channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Pushes to Restart Peace Talks, Open to Any Venue Except Russia, Belarus
item.description: President Zelensky said Ukraine is ready to meet with Russian representatives in any country except Russia or Belarus, calling for resumption of U.S.-mediated talks. The appeal comes as peace negotiations have remained frozen for nearly two months, with Washington shifting focus to the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Approves 20th Russia Sanctions Package After Hungary, Slovakia Drop Veto
item.description: EU ambassadors agreed on the 20th package of sanctions against Moscow after Hungary and Slovakia lifted their veto. The move follows Orban election defeat and paves way for a 90-billion-euro loan for Kyiv. Ukraine also resumed oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Moldova Oligarch Plahotniuc Sentenced to 19 Years in Prison
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, once Moldova most powerful oligarch who de facto controlled the country government in the 2010s, was sentenced to 19 years for embezzlement, fraud and money laundering in connection with the 2014 theft of billion from three Moldovan banks. His fall marks Moldova alignment with European democratic values.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Praises African Nations for Blocking Taiwan President Flight
item.description: China expressed "high appreciation" for Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar after they denied overflight rights to Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te plane, forcing cancellation of his trip to Eswatini. Beijing denied applying economic pressure while praising the countries for supporting the one-China principle.

item.title: ⚔️ US Seizes Iranian-Linked Ship in Indian Ocean, Extends Ceasefire Deadline
item.description: U.S. forces boarded the sanctioned oil tanker M/T Tifani in the Indian Ocean, marking the first interdiction outside the Middle East since Operation Epic Fury began. Trump announced an extended ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan request while maintaining the blockade, as Iranian officials rejected the terms.

item.title: 🚢⚠️ Iran Fires on Three Ships in Strait of Hormuz as US Blockade Continues
item.description: Iranian forces targeted three vessels in the strategic waterway, including one that was heavily damaged, as Tehran escalates pressure following the US naval blockade and seized cargo ship.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Approves €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine as Druzhba Pipeline Restarts
item.description: The massive loan package comes as Ukraine reopens the Druzhba oil pipeline, ending a months-long stalemate over stalled supplies to Hungary and signaling continued Western support.

item.title: 🛢️🇷🇺 Russia to Halt Oil Flow to Germany via Druzhba Pipeline from May 1
item.description: Moscow confirmed it will suspend shipments of Kazakh oil to Germany through the pipeline, adding another layer of energy disruption to European markets already strained by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan President Cancels Trip After African Countries Revoke Flight Permits
item.description: Taiwan accused Beijing of pressuring African nations to close their airspace to President Lai Ching-te’s plane, the latest in a series of Chinese efforts to isolate Taiwan diplomatically.

item.title: 🇳🇬⚖️ Nigerian Coup Plot Suspects Deny Treason Charges in Court
item.description: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled, marking the latest political turmoil in Africa’s most populous nation.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Eyes Mediator Role in US-Iran Conflict as Singh Signals Diplomatic Opening
item.description: Defense Minister Rajnath Singh suggested India may take on a larger peacemaker role in the US-Iran conflict during a visit to Berlin, highlighting Modis balanced diplomatic posture.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore Faces Threefold Governance Test From Iran War Fallout
item.description: The Straits Times analysis examines how Singapore must manage immediate disruptions while navigating ongoing geopolitical turmoil and uncertainty from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵 China-Japan Flight Rift Deepens as 2,700 Flights Cancelled in March
item.description: Nearly half of planned flights between China and Japan were cancelled last month as Beijing and Tokyo remain locked in tensions, signaling deteriorating bilateral relations.

item.title: 🌾 Asia Braces for Food Shortages as El Nino and Iran War Effects Collide
item.description: A precarious planting season looms as suppressed rainfall and soaring fertilizer costs threaten harvests across the region, with Iran war disruptions compounding El Nino impacts.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China Praises African Countries for Blocking Taiwan Presidents Flight
item.description: Beijing welcomed the decision by three African countries to revoke permission for Taiwan presidents plane to use their airspace, forcing cancellation of a planned trip to Eswatini.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Charts Own Path in Israel Talks Separating From Iran
item.description: Lebanon is seeking to distance itself from Iran in potential negotiations with Israel, with officials saying We cannot let Iran be our spokesperson in regional diplomacy.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF Kills Two Hezbollah Operatives Who Crossed Ceasefire Line in Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli Air Force struck and killed two Hezbollah operatives who crossed the ceasefire line in southern Lebanon, amid ongoing tensions on the northern border.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Woman and Child Killed in Russian City After Ukrainian Drone Strike
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack caused an apartment building to partially collapse in the Russian city of Syzran, killing a woman and child and injuring 12 others.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Drafts Entry Ban on Former Russian Combatants Targeting War Veterans
item.description: EU High Representative Kaja Kallas proposed restricting former Russian combatants entry to the bloc by Junes European Council, with Ukraines foreign minister endorsing the plan.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇹🇷 Ukraine Asks Turkey to Host Zelenskyy-Putin Peace Talks
item.description: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Ukraine had approached Turkey and other capitals about hosting potential peace talks between Zelenskyy and Putin.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Launches Massive Drone and Missile Attack on Ukraine, Killing 16
item.description: Russian forces targeted civilian areas across Ukraine with nearly 700 drones and dozens of ballistic and cruise missiles in the worst aerial attack in weeks. Ukrainian officials acknowledged interceptor stockpiles are running critically low.

item.title: 🕊️ Key Takeaways After Iran-US-Israel Ceasefire Reached
item.description: A tentative two-week ceasefire has paused hostilities between Iran, Israel, and the United States, but significant challenges remain for a permanent deal. Major differences persist between Trump and Iran's surviving leaders following Khamenei's death.

item.title: ⚖️ Trump Global Tariffs Face Fresh Legal Challenge in Trade Court
item.description: Trump's temporary global import tariffs are under renewed legal assault in the U.S. Court of International Trade after the Supreme Court struck down his broader IEEPA-based tariffs in February.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Accelerates NATO Fallback Plan in Case of US Withdrawal
item.description: European officials are advancing contingency plans dubbed "European NATO" to preserve defense capabilities if the U.S. pulls out. Germany, previously opposed to go-it-alone approaches, has now signed on.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskyy Seeks More Arms Deals With European Allies
item.description: Ukraine's president is visiting three European capitals in 48 hours to secure military and financial support. Germany agreed to a 4 billion euro defense package while Norway pledged 9 billion euros in assistance.
